layman_headline: Market fear is high but no clean trade setup exists right now — staying on the sidelines.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with Bitcoin dominance high at 56.4%, meaning money is flowing to Bitcoin rather than altcoins. Most altcoin candidates are either already overbought after big runs (KAITO up 47%, DEXE, LDO) or in freefall with no signs of stopping (BONK down 27%). The best potential setup is Ethereum near oversold levels, but it's still in a downtrend below all moving averages, so we need to wait for a clearer reversal signal before committing money.
layman_action: Wait and watch. Don't buy anything yet. If Ethereum drops to $1,800 support and bounces with increasing volume, that could be the entry. Patience pays in fearful markets.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a clean, high-conviction swing entry right now. BONK (RSI 31) has a strongly bearish chart (-2.0 bias) with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu — classic don't-catch-a-falling-knife setup. KAITO and LDO are already extended (RSI 79 and 59 respectively) with poor risk/reward ratios (0.46x and 0.38x). TRUMP at RSI 22 is deeply oversold but the 1.0x R/R and persistent downtrend make it a coin flip. ETH is the most compelling watch — RSI 39 approaching oversold (our most reliable signal at 80% win rate), 5-day momentum turning positive, and Blockworks noting a derivatives reset. However, the chart bias is still -0.70 with price below all MAs, so we need confirmation via a bounce off $1,800 support before entering. High BTC dominance (56.4%) is a headwind for all alts.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.4% continues to drain capital from altcoins — any alt position faces structural headwinds
Fear & Greed at 28 is low but not at extreme capitulation levels (sub-15) that historically mark reliable bottoms
Wall Street's $128B private credit exposure concern could trigger broader risk-off if it escalates
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a strong bounce with volume would trigger a BUY signal
BTC dominance trend — if it starts declining from 56.4%, altcoins get tailwind for recovery trades
Fear & Greed index — a move below 20 would signal capitulation and stronger mean-reversion opportunity
market_context: Crypto markets are in a fear state (28/100) with BTC holding steady near $64,335 while altcoins bleed. High BTC dominance at 56.4% signals this is not yet an alt-friendly environment, and most oversold altcoins lack clear reversal signals.
context_banner: Fear-driven market with high BTC dominance offers no clean alt entries yet — discipline over FOMO.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but needs to reach sub-35 or show clear bounce for entry confirmation.
Fear & Greed at 28 indicates fear but not extreme capitulation; historically this zone produces mixed results (62% win rate), suggesting patience over aggressive buying.
Blockworks reporting 'ETH derivatives reset and the next retail trade' suggests positioning is clearing, potentially setting up for a cleaner move higher.
BONK's Three Black Crows and -2.0 chart bias confirm catching falling knives is dangerous; LDO's Three White Soldiers (+0.85 bias) are bullish but the 0.38x R/R makes entry unattractive.
FTX distributing $900M to creditors in fifth payment round could inject liquidity back into crypto markets over coming weeks — a macro tailwind to monitor.
